title: Bankruptcy Allocation: Fair Shares in Tough Times

intro.text: Welcome to the world of bankruptcy allocation, where we explore how to fairly distribute limited resources among claimants. This guide will help you understand various principles and rules used to ensure equitable distribution. You'll learn about different methods like the Contested Garment Principle and the Talmud Rule, and why these concepts matter in real-world financial scenarios.

highlights.label: Constrained Equal Awards
highlights.desc: Equalizing awards until claims are met

sections.title: Contested Garment Principle (Splitting the Difference)
sections.content: The Contested Garment Principle (CGP) is a method used when there are two claimants. It divides the estate into uncontested and contested parts, giving the uncontested part to the larger claim and splitting the contested part equally.

subsections.title: Two-Claimant Case
subsections.content: In a scenario with two claimants, the estate is divided as follows:

sections.title: Constrained Equal Awards (Equalizing Until Exhausted)
sections.content: Constrained Equal Awards (CEA) aims to equalize the awards given to claimants until the total claims are met. This method uses a water-filling approach, raising all claims equally until the estate is exhausted.

subsections.title: Water-Filling Method
bullets: xi = min(ci, t), where Σmin(ci, t) = E

sections.title: Constrained Equal Losses (Starting from Full Claims)
sections.content: Constrained Equal Losses (CEL) focuses on equalizing losses until some claims reach zero. It starts from full claims and subtracts equally until the estate fits.

subsections.title: Loss Equalization
bullets: xi = max(0, ci - t), where Σmax(ci - t, 0) = E

sections.title: Proportional Rule (Simple and Quick)
sections.content: The Proportional Rule (PROP) is a straightforward method that allocates the estate proportionally to the claims. It's quick and easy to apply.

sections.title: Talmud Rule (A Hybrid Approach)
sections.content: The Talmud Rule (TAL) is a hybrid method combining CEA and CEL on half-claims. It provides a balanced approach by applying different rules to different parts of the claims.

subsections.title: Half-Claims Approach
subsections.content: The Talmud Rule works as follows:
bullets: Case 2: E ≥ (1/2)C → give hi first, then CEL on second halves

sections.title: Axioms (Fairness Tests)
sections.content: Axioms are fairness tests that ensure the allocation methods adhere to principles like efficiency, equal treatment, consistency, and monotonicity.

subsections.title: Key Fairness Principles
bullets: Equal Treatment of Equals (ETE): equal claims → equal awards
Consistency: removing one claimant doesn’t change others’ shares
Monotonicity: higher claims don’t get less than lower claims
